title: Is Shinji a pretty boy
wikitext:
[]: Is Shinji a "pretty boy"? Post by Barinax » Tue Nov 04, 2008 9:58 pm Well, I don't even know what a pretty boy is. But I've seen people who are called pretty boys, people who call themselves pretty boys, and... other people who aren't called pretty and don't think of themselves as pretty but nonetheless would be seen as a pretty boy by... someone. Just look at urban dictionary http://www.urbandictionary.com/define.php?term=pretty+boy Okay, well... this was just a random thought.

[]: Post by Sachi » Tue Nov 04, 2008 11:04 pm Not at all. -Shinji doesn't dress well. He's always wearing his school uniform. -He doesn't care much about his appearance (at least from what we see in the show), and he doesn't constantly check the mirror to fix his hair. -And is he even considered "pretty"? You yourself can't tell by looking at a picture. So overal, no.

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Nov 05, 2008 12:39 am Now hold on here I wear my school cloths every where more or less and they are more or less Ikaris....And I mean I check the mirror but some times you don't have a lot of time. ;___; What eva, the con girls still dig it ^__^ ...I'm just really bored and needed to post. argh

[]: Post by chee » Wed Nov 05, 2008 2:54 am If episode 5 is anything to go by, the girls at his school seem to like him. (The fact that several female protagonists may or may not want to ride his joystick should be taken into account as well.)

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Nov 05, 2008 3:38 am Lawl...Joystick.. Its up to the fan base, Shinji fans say yes but if your not a fan then nu.

[]: Post by NemZ » Wed Nov 05, 2008 3:45 am He may not be narcissistic but Shinji definately is a pretty boy. Shinji definately dresses better than his friends. The neat freak in him means he almost certainly has not only clean but ironed clothes every day. I'd assume that he also is rather fastidious about his hygiene. As to just raw looks, all you need to see is his windblown look in ep4 to get that he's a bishi. A polite, well groomed, potential hero with a tragic past from a powerful family... if he looked up from his navel occasionally he'd have been the king of that school.

[]: Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Wed Nov 05, 2008 6:25 am If I were a girl (I have a penis) I would say Shinji would look kinda cute (I'm still a male, in case you've forgotten) considering his quiet personality...and style. Come on, you gotta admit, the clothes he wears are pretty damn cute-looking.

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Nov 05, 2008 6:52 am His cloths are normal school cloths, kind I wear every day more or less. You know what I hate, when people toss on a white dress shirt and a blue undershirt and call it "cosplaying". That is not a cosplay. My 2 cents..

[]: Post by Sachi » Wed Nov 05, 2008 8:58 am He may be "pretty" by all standards, but he's no where near a "pretty boy". Being a "pretty boy" is more like being a meterosexual, and Shinji isn't going to far lengths like getting manicures or wearing makeup to make himself look pretty. He cares about how he looks, but to an extent which doesn't make him a "pretty boy". And even his OCD nature wouldn't come close to making him one. Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: You know what I hate, when people toss on a white dress shirt and a blue undershirt and call it "cosplaying". That is not a cosplay.

[]: Post by BrikHaus » Wed Nov 05, 2008 9:41 am Shinji is a mama's boy. Isn't it obvious?

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Nov 05, 2008 10:08 am Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: You know what I hate, when people toss on a white dress shirt and a blue undershirt and call it "cosplaying". That is not a cosplay.

[]: Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Nov 05, 2008 10:20 am Must I point out his pink t-shirt. And the running away sequence on the suicide point with his hair blowing in the wind like he is some kind of male model.

[]: Post by V » Wed Nov 05, 2008 12:34 pm under your definition (which seems close to "metrosexual"), no **fun fact: the character design for Shinji is literally, taking the head of the female character Nadia from Gainax's earlier series, "Nadia and the Secret of Blue Water", cutting her bangs short, and slapping it onto a male body. Nadia was also dark-skinned so they changed that too; but otherwise, they literally took a girl's head, minus the jewelry and shortened the hair a bit. He thus still has a proportionately longer neck, and overall effeminate look. This was not done to make him a "pretty boy", so much as part of the overall theme of emasculating him; making him as un-masculine as possible; this is part of showing how he's a failure at pretty much everything (he's not tough like he should be)

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Nov 05, 2008 1:55 pm its not pink, its light red or Maroon

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Nov 05, 2008 1:56 pm What chee said and also: http://evacommentary.org/episode-04/episode-04A-scene2B.html#cut085 Bishounen (beautiful boy) Nuff said. I'd say he is, although he's not into fashion/constantly checking himself out.

[]: Post by Reichu » Wed Nov 05, 2008 2:35 pm Shinji's a bishounen because I said so? Well, I guess I can deal with that.

[]: Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Nov 05, 2008 2:36 pm Hey, SSD, thanks for the link! That is the scene I mentioned.

[]: Post by Reichu » Wed Nov 05, 2008 2:53 pm Action_Bastard wrote: Must I point out his pink t-shirt.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Nov 05, 2008 3:03 pm @ Reichu: Not just "because you said so" but he certainly LOOKS like a bishounen. Personality-wise, I really can't recall if anime pretty boys act a certain way or not, but I do think Shinji fits the bill even if he doesn't act like the norm.

[]: Post by Hunter21 » Wed Nov 05, 2008 5:11 pm BrikHaus wrote: Shinji is a mama's boy. Isn't it obvious?
